# UC-14.10.5 · Aranet Temperature vs CO2 Correlation as Occupancy Proxy

## Description

CO2 rises with occupancy while temperature may lag; a rolling CO₂-per-degree intensity metric highlights stale-air zones independent of thermostat readings alone.

## Value

Informs demand-controlled ventilation and space planning.

## Implementation

(1) Stable clock sync on sensors. (2) Minimum sample per window. (3) Review outliers after holidays.

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="aranet:sensor" earliest=-7d
| eval temp=tonumber(coalesce(temp_c, ""))
| eval co2=tonumber(coalesce(co2_ppm, ""))
| where isnotnull(temp) AND isnotnull(co2)
| bucket _time span=15m
| stats avg(temp) as temp_c avg(co2) as co2_ppm by _time location
| eval co2_per_deg=if(temp_c!=0, co2_ppm/temp_c, null())
| stats avg(co2_per_deg) as co2_intensity stdev(co2_per_deg) as variability by location
| where co2_intensity>30
```

## Visualization

Bar chart of co2_intensity by wing; table of high-variability rooms.

## References

- [Aranet — environmental monitoring](https://aranet.com/)
- [ASHRAE — IAQ](https://www.ashrae.org/)
- [OSHA indoor air](https://www.osha.gov/indoor-air-quality)
